# Geo-locate destination IPs with renamed columns sorted by country

**Dataset**: `gx_11_ext`

This variant of the geo-locate pattern renames the default `iploc` output columns inline using `loc_country as country, loc_city as city`, producing analyst-friendly field names instead of the verbose `loc_` prefixes. The result is projected with `fields` and ordered with `sort asc country` so connections cluster alphabetically by country for quick triage. Gotcha: the rename clauses (`<geo_col> as <alias>`) go directly on the `iploc` stage after the source IP argument — they are not a separate `alter` stage.

```sql
dataset = gx_11_ext
| filter dst_ip != null
| iploc dst_ip loc_country as country, loc_city as city
| fields dst_ip, country, city
| sort asc country
```
